data.title: Critical Metals Corp. stock (CRML) surged 21% after announcing 4 rare earth off-take term sheets, and business webcast.
data.description: Shares of Critical Metals Corp. (NASDAQ: CRML) saw a significant increase on Thursday following a business update shared through a conference call. CRML stock surged by approximately 21% during midday trading, driven by a mix of geopolitical factors and internal company news. A key announcement was the disclosure of four non-binding term sheets for the off-take of rare earth concentrates, securing the allocation of 100% of the Tanbreez project's expected production. In a webcast held at 8:00 a.m. ET on Thursday, management offered insights into the company's developments. The surge in stock price further indicates positive investor sentiment in response to these updates.

data.title: JBL introduces JBL BandBox, an AI-powered amp and speaker for musicians. Includes JBL BandBox Solo and JBL BandBox Trio.
data.description: JBL has introduced the JBL BandBox, a new line of AI-powered smart practice amps and portable speakers that cater to both beginner and experienced musicians. The JBL BandBox Solo and JBL BandBox Trio models offer real-time vocal and instrument separation using AI technology, along with signature JBL sound quality and a range of built-in effects and practice tools. These devices combine the convenience of portable audio with innovative features tailored specifically for musicians.

data.title: Belgium Data Center Colocation market worth $220M in 2024, expected to reach $388M by 2030 with a growth rate of 9.92%.
data.description: A report on the Belgium Data Center Colocation Market has been integrated into ResearchAndMarkets.com's portfolio, indicating a value of USD 220 million in 2024. Predictions suggest the market will grow to reach USD 388 million by 2030, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 9.92%. Belgium stands as an up-and-coming market for data center colocation in Western Europe, currently home to around 34 operational colocation data centers.

data.title: Transflo introduces Workflow AI for LTL, a platform resolving exceptions swiftly to enhance cash flow.
data.description: An automation platform called Workflow AI for LTL has been introduced by Transflo in the less-than-truckload sector in Tampa. This platform aims to quickly address exceptions and enhance cash flow for businesses operating in this space.

data.title: NVIDIA DRIVE AV boosts Mercedes-Benz CLA to highest Euro NCAP safety rating, winning Best Performer 2025 with AI technology.
data.description: The Mercedes-Benz CLA has been awarded the Best Performer of 2025 by Euro NCAP, partly due to its use of the NVIDIA DRIVE AV software. This award highlights a movement towards artificial intelligence-driven safety norms in vehicles. This recognition signifies a significant step towards safer driving with the integration of advanced technology.

data.title: ATP Flight School leads airline placements in 2025 with 983 graduates; American Airlines hires most ATP graduates.
data.description: In 2025, ATP Flight School in Jacksonville, Florida, emerged as a top leader in placing graduates in the airline industry with 983 individuals securing positions at regional and major airlines, along with corporate operators. Among the major airlines, American Airlines stood out by hiring the largest number of ATP graduates.

data.title: Ripple CEO supports CLARITY Act for crypto regulation despite its flaws, says 'we're so close we can't give up now.'
data.description: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse expressed his optimism about the CLARITY Act, stating that "we're so close we can't give up now" in response to the delayed crypto bill following the withdrawal of support from Coinbase Global Inc. Despite acknowledging flaws, Garlinghouse defended the bill as a "massive step forward," emphasizing the importance of regulatory clarity over chaos. This comes after Ripple's prolonged legal battle with the SEC regarding XRP. Garlinghouse highlighted that while the bill may not be perfect, he believes that striving for perfection can impede progress. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cohen & Co Community Foundation gives 28 grants totaling $100,000 to not-for-profits in inaugural year.
data.description: In Cleveland on January 22, 2026, Cohen & Co, a top accounting firm, revealed the beneficiaries of its first-ever Grant Application Program, supported by the Cohen & Co Community Foundation. The Foundation allocated $100,000 in 2025 to 28 non-profit organizations.

data.title: Psyence BioMed adjourns annual shareholders meeting to February 12, 2026.
data.description: On January 22, 2026, Psyence Biomedical Ltd. (Nasdaq: PBM) postponed its annual and special meeting of shareholders until February 12, 2026.

data.description: The cryptocurrency Dogwifhat (WIF) is currently priced at $0.34 per token. Technical analysis indicates a possible 6% increase to reach the $0.36 resistance level in the upcoming week. The short-term target for WIF within the next week is expected to see a rise to $0.36.

data.title: Robert Half recognized by Fortune as one of the World's Most Admired Companies for 29th consecutive year. $RHI.
data.description: A prestigious global talent solutions and business consulting firm, Robert Half, based in Menlo Park, California, along with its subsidiary Protiviti®, has earned the noteworthy recognition of being named one of the 2026 World's Most Admired Companies by Fortune. This achievement highlights Robert Half's position as part of an exclusive group of esteemed companies recognized for their excellence in various business sectors. The firm, listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol RHI, continues to demonstrate its commitment to exceptional service and industry leadership.

data.title: BGL arranged $135M in financing for Hall of Fame Village waterpark and hotel projects in Cleveland.
data.description: $135 million in real estate financing has been structured by Brown Gibbons Lang & Company (BGL) to progress waterpark and hotel projects. BGL, a prominent independent investment bank, revealed this development on Jan. 22, 2026.

data.title: ADM (NYSE: ADM) named one of world's most admired food production companies in FORTUNE 2026 list. Developed by FORTUNE and Korn Ferry, from 1,500 candidates.
data.description: ADM (NYSE: ADM) has been recognized as one of the top companies in the food production industry for 2026 by FORTUNE Magazine. This prestigious accolade places ADM among the world's most admired companies in the sector. The list, compiled by FORTUNE in collaboration with Korn Ferry, is a comprehensive assessment of corporate reputation. The selection process involved evaluating approximately 1,500 candidates, including the 1,000 largest U.S. companies based on revenue, as well as international firms listed in the Fortune Global 500 database.

data.title: Alpenrose closing manufacturing facility in Clackamas, OR, to consolidate ops; Fresh milk production continues in Kent, Wash.
data.description: Alpenrose has announced plans to consolidate operations by shutting down its butter, sour cream, and ice cream mix manufacturing plant in Clackamas, OR. This decision, effective from March 31, 2026, will impact production at the facility. However, the production of fresh milk will carry on at the Pacific Northwest facility in Kent, Wash. This move ensures that Alpenrose's popular milk and cottage cheese products will still be accessible in local grocery stores throughout the region. To assist in this transition, Alpenrose has enlisted the services of Harry Davis & Company.

data.title: CEO Paul Markovich urges Congress to address healthcare system failures causing high costs and lack of coverage for Americans.
data.description: On January 22, 2026, it was reported that the president and CEO of Ascendiun, the parent company of Blue Shield of California, Paul Markovich, highlighted the serious issues in the healthcare system. Markovich emphasized that the systemic failures have led to exorbitant costs and a large number of Americans lacking access to affordable insurance. This revelation underscores the critical need for reform and improvement within the healthcare sector to address the growing concerns faced by individuals and families across the country.

data.title: BrainTap had 2.2M sessions and 46.6M minutes listened in 2025. New ski-focused series ignites 2026.
data.description: Kicking off 2026 with a fresh ski-focused series is Dr. Tom Lankering in New Bern, North Carolina. BrainTap, a top player in audio-guided brainwave entrainment and mental wellness tech, recently unveiled their 2025 year-end figures. The report highlights extraordinary achievements and impressive progress, hinting at a record-breaking performance.
